senses.definition: Was made to feel hungry or interested in food.
examples: The smell of fresh bread appetized the tired travelers.
She was already appetized by the aromas coming from the kitchen.

senses.definition: The act or process of making someone feel hungry or eager to eat.
examples: The cook believed that presentation was an important part of appetizement.
Soft music and candlelight contributed to the appetizement of the guests.

senses.definition: A small dish of food eaten before the main part of a meal.
examples: We ordered a salad as an appetizer before the main course.
The restaurant offers a free appetizer with every drink order.

senses.definition: Small dishes of food eaten before the main part of a meal.
examples: Drinks and appetizers will be served at 6 p.m.
They shared several appetizers instead of ordering main dishes.

senses.definition: Makes someone feel hungry or eager to eat.
examples: The smell of garlic appetizes everyone who walks past the restaurant.
Just looking at the menu appetizes me.

senses.definition: Looking or smelling so good that you want to eat or drink it.
examples: The soup looks very appetizing with all those fresh herbs.
An appetizing smell came from the kitchen.

senses.definition: Attractive or interesting in a way that makes you want it, not only for food.
examples: The offer of extra vacation days sounded quite appetizing.
They presented an appetizing plan for future investment.

senses.definition: In a way that makes you want to eat or drink something.
examples: The vegetables were arranged appetizingly on the plate.
The bread was appetizingly warm and fresh.

senses.definition: A type of dark igneous rock containing minerals such as hornblende and biotite.
examples: The geologists collected several samples of appinite from the mountain.
Appinite often appears as dark, coarse-grained dikes in older rocks.

senses.definition: A Roman given name, especially known from historical figures in ancient Rome.
examples: Appius Claudius was an important Roman statesman.
The story mentions a senator named Appius.

senses.definition: Written short form for words like 'applied' or 'application', used in technical or academic contexts.
examples: The data were taken from J. Appl. Phys., a physics journal.
Please send your CV to the HR appl. address listed below.

senses.definition: Flat or flattened, especially used to describe the shape of a surface in science.
examples: Under the microscope, the insect's eyes appeared applanate.
The coral has an applanate form pressed close to the rock.

senses.definition: The act of flattening a surface, especially the eye, to measure pressure or thickness.
examples: Applanation of the cornea is used to measure eye pressure.
The device works by gentle applanation of the eye's surface.

senses.definition: To show you enjoyed a performance or speech by clapping your hands.
examples: The audience began to applaud loudly at the end of the show.
Everyone stood up to applaud the singer.

senses.definition: To praise or approve of someone's action or idea.
examples: I applaud your decision to continue your studies.
Many people applaud the government's new policy.

senses.definition: Deserving praise or approval.
examples: Their efforts to reduce waste are highly applaudable.
It was an applaudable attempt to improve the system.

senses.definition: In a way that deserves praise or approval.
examples: The company has acted applaudably in addressing the problem.
He handled the crisis applaudably and calmly.

senses.definition: Clapped your hands to show that you enjoyed a performance or speech.
examples: The crowd applauded when the band came back on stage.
Everyone applauded at the end of his speech.

senses.definition: Praised or approved of someone's action or idea.
examples: The proposal was widely applauded by experts.
Her courage has been applauded by many commentators.

senses.definition: A person who claps or shows approval for someone or something.
examples: The loudest applauder sat in the front row.
She has long been an enthusiastic applauder of the arts.

senses.definition: People who clap or show approval for someone or something.
examples: The applauders filled the hall with noise.
Her supporters were loud applauders of the new policy.

senses.definition: Clapping your hands to show that you enjoyed a performance or speech.
examples: People were still applauding as the curtain fell.
The audience kept applauding for several minutes.

senses.definition: Praising or approving of someone's action or idea.
examples: Many are applauding the company for its transparency.
Commentators are applauding the new agreement.

senses.definition: In a way that shows praise or approval, often by clapping.
examples: The crowd responded applaudingly to the final song.
She nodded applaudingly at the suggestion.

senses.definition: Claps hands to show that someone enjoyed a performance or speech.
examples: The audience always applauds when she finishes singing.
He smiles as the crowd applauds.

senses.definition: Praises or approves of someone's action or idea.
examples: The committee applauds your efforts.
Everyone applauds the decision to make the data public.

senses.definition: The sound of many people clapping their hands to show they enjoyed something.
examples: The singer walked off stage to loud applause.
There was a burst of applause when the winner was announced.

senses.definition: Public praise or approval for someone's actions or ideas.
examples: The policy won applause from environmental groups.
Her efforts have earned widespread applause.

senses.definition: Separate acts or bursts of applause.
examples: There were several applauses during the concert.
The speech was interrupted by repeated applauses.

senses.definition: Showing or expressing praise and approval.
examples: He received applausive comments from the reviewers.
The tone of the article is strongly applausive.

senses.definition: In a way that shows praise and approval.
examples: The crowd reacted applausively to his performance.
The proposal was applausively received by the board.

senses.definition: A round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin and a firm white inside.
examples: She ate an apple for a snack.
We picked fresh apples from the tree.

senses.definition: A tree that produces apples.
examples: There is an old apple in the middle of the garden.
They planted several apples along the fence.

senses.definition: A small berry-like fruit, or a plant producing such fruit, named for its apple-like taste or smell.
examples: The children picked appleberry from the bushes near the river.
Appleberry jam is a local specialty in that region.

senses.definition: The flower of an apple tree.
examples: In spring, the air smelled of appleblossom.
The hillside was white with appleblossom.

senses.definition: A cart used for carrying or selling apples, especially in the street.
examples: The vendor pushed his applecart into the market square.
An old applecart stood outside the shop.

senses.definition: Used in the idiom 'upset the applecart', meaning a plan or situation that can be easily disturbed.
examples: One small mistake could upset the whole applecart.
They didn’t want anything to upset the applecart before the deal was signed.

senses.definition: Carts used for carrying or selling apples, or figuratively, plans or arrangements that can be disturbed.
examples: Fruit sellers lined the street with their applecarts.
Several political applecarts were upset by the sudden announcement.

senses.definition: Having apples, covered with apples, or marked with apple-like spots.
examples: An appled branch hung low over the path.
The field was full of appled trees ready for harvest.

senses.definition: A dialect word for a wasp.
examples: She screamed when an appledrain flew near her face.
There were several appledrains buzzing around the fallen fruit.

senses.definition: Dialect word for wasps, used in the plural.
examples: The garden was full of appledrains in late summer.
They were careful not to disturb the appledrains near the shed.

senses.definition: A dialect or variant form of 'appledrain', meaning a wasp.
examples: An old poem mentions an 'appledrane' circling the orchard.
He was stung by an appledrane while picking fruit.

senses.definition: A dialect or rare variant word referring to a wasp or similar stinging insect.
examples: Children were warned not to tease an appledrone.
An appledrone hovered over the ripe apples.

senses.definition: A person or company that grows apples as a business.
examples: The region depends heavily on local applegrowers.
She comes from a family of applegrowers.

senses.definition: A strong alcoholic drink made from apples, especially by distilling or freezing cider.
examples: He ordered a glass of applejack at the bar.
Traditional applejack was made on local farms.

senses.definition: A type of sweet breakfast cereal or snack made with apple flavor and sugar.
examples: The kids like to eat applejacks for breakfast.
She bought a box of applejacks at the supermarket.

senses.definition: A type of apple kept until it becomes shriveled and very ripe, formerly used for cooking.
examples: The recipe calls for an old applejohn for richer flavor.
They stored applejohns in the cellar over the winter.

senses.definition: A person who sells apples, especially in a market.
examples: The applemonger shouted out the price of his fruit.
An old applemonger worked at the corner of the street.

senses.definition: A person who is extremely fond of apples, or a playful term for such a fan.
examples: He calls himself an applenut and tries every new variety.
Only an applenut would drive that far for a rare apple.

senses.definition: A dialect name for the herb southernwood, a fragrant plant once grown in gardens.
examples: They planted appleringie along the garden path.
A faint smell of appleringie hung in the old house.

senses.definition: Dialect plural form referring to plants of southernwood.
examples: Old gardens were once full of appleringies.
They brushed past the appleringies and released a strong scent.

senses.definition: A dialect variant of 'appleringie', meaning the herb southernwood.
examples: She remembers the smell of appleringy from her grandmother's garden.
Appleringy was traditionally used to scent clothes.

senses.definition: The root of an apple tree, or a plant root that smells or tastes like apple.
examples: The old appleroot spread under most of the garden.
Some herbalists use dried appleroot in their remedies.

senses.definition: Round fruits with red, green, or yellow skin and a firm white inside.
examples: We bought some apples at the market.
She sliced the apples for the pie.

senses.definition: A smooth sauce made by cooking apples, usually sweetened and sometimes spiced.
examples: We served roast pork with applesauce.
She made homemade applesauce for the children.

senses.definition: Mainly US, informal: something considered nonsense or silly talk.
examples: That excuse is pure applesauce.
He said it was all applesauce and refused to believe it.

senses.definition: Different types or servings of applesauce.
examples: The store sells several applesauces with no added sugar.
She tried a few applesauces before choosing her favorite.

senses.definition: Slices or pieces of dried apple, used especially in traditional cooking.
examples: They stored applesnits for use during the winter.
The recipe calls for a cup of applesnits.

senses.definition: A small program that runs inside another larger program or on a web page.
examples: The chart is generated by a Java applet.
You can download a simple applet to test your connection.
